---
title: "ECON 444 Consulting Project"
author: "Kendall Landwehr & Tatum Barkat"
format: html
---

```{r}
#Reading in data 

install.packages("readxl")

library(readxl)
consumption_data <- read_excel("data/2021-2025_consumption_data.xlsx")

price_data <- read_excel("data/2021-2025_price_data.xlsx")

#Renaming columns
library(dplyr)

consumption_data <- consumption_data %>%
  rename(Hour_DK = `Time(DK)`)
consumption_data <- consumption_data %>%
  rename(Consumption_kWh = `Consumption(kWh)`)
price_data <- price_data %>%
  rename(Spot_Price_DKK = `Spot Price (DKK)`)
price_data <- price_data %>%
  rename(Hour_DK = `Hour (DK)`)

class(consumption_data$Hour_DK)

#Adjusting time zone data
consumption_data$time <- as.POSIXct(consumption_data$Hour_DK,
                                    format = "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S",
                                    tz = "Europe/Copenhagen")

price_data$time <- as.POSIXct(price_data$Hour_DK,
                              format = "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S",
                              tz = "Europe/Copenhagen")

#Checks
class(consumption_data$time)
attr(consumption_data$time, "tzone")
```

```{r}
#Merging data sets

library(dplyr)

merged_data <- left_join(consumption_data,
                         price_data,
                         by = c("time", "Region"))
#Deleting unnecessary hour columns

merged_data <- merged_data %>%
  select(-Hour_DK.x, -Hour_DK.y)

#Renaming time column and moving to left
merged_data <- merged_data %>%
  rename (Time = time)
merged_data <- merged_data %>%
  select(Time, everything())
```

```{r}
# Fit the regression model
model <- lm(Consumption_kWh ~ Spot_Price_DKK, data = merged_data)

# View a summary of results
summary(model)

# Plot residuals vs fitted values
plot(model, which = 1)
```


```{r}
# Normal Q-Q plot to check residuals
plot(model, which = 2)

library(ggplot2)

ggplot(merged_data, aes(x = Spot_Price_DKK, y = Consumption_kWh)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", col = "blue") +
  labs(x = "Spot Price (DKK)", y = "Consumption (kWh)",
       title = "Consumption vs Spot Price")
```

```{r}
#Divide consumption by 1000

merged_data <- merged_data %>%
  mutate(Consumption_MWh = Consumption_kWh / 1000)
model_MWh <- lm(Consumption_MWh ~ Spot_Price_DKK,
                data = merged_data)

summary(model_MWh)

library(ggplot2)

ggplot(merged_data, aes(x = Spot_Price_DKK, y = Consumption_MWh)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3, color = "steelblue") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "red") +
  labs(
    title = "Electricity Consumption vs Spot Price",
    x = "Spot Price (DKK)",
    y = "Consumption (MWh)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```

```{r}
#Divide graph by sector

library(ggplot2)

ggplot(merged_data,
       aes(x = Spot_Price_DKK,
           y = Consumption_MWh)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.2, color = "steelblue") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "red") +
  facet_wrap(~ Sector) +
  labs(
    title = "Hourly Consumption vs Price by Sector",
    x = "Spot Price (DKK)",
    y = "Consumption (MWh)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
#Transparency and smaller points to reduce overplotting
ggplot(merged_data,
       aes(x = Spot_Price_DKK,
           y = Consumption_MWh,
           color = Sector)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.05, size = 0.4) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, linewidth = 1) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

```{r}
# Checking for percentage of negatives

mean(merged_data$Spot_Price_DKK < 0, na.rm = TRUE) * 100

merged_data %>%
  group_by(Sector) %>%
  summarise(
    negative_count = sum(Spot_Price_DKK < 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    total_obs = n(),
    share_negative = mean(Spot_Price_DKK < 0, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
```

```{r}
# Test log-log model with negative values removed

filtered_data <- merged_data %>%
  filter(Spot_Price_DKK > 0)

ggplot(filtered_data,
       aes(x = Spot_Price_DKK,
           y = Consumption_MWh,
           color = Sector)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.05, size = 0.4) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  labs(
    title = "Hourly Consumption vs Price (Log-Log, negatives removed)",
    x = "Spot Price (log scale)",
    y = "Consumption (log scale)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
#Aggregate by hour for visualization

library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)

hourly_summary <- merged_data %>%
  mutate(hour = hour(Time)) %>%
  group_by(Sector, hour) %>%
  summarise(
    avg_consumption = mean(Consumption_MWh, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_price = mean(Spot_Price_DKK, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

#Plot with regression fitted on raw data

library(ggplot2)

ggplot() +
  # Regression line from raw hourly data
  geom_smooth(
    data = merged_data,                          # raw hourly data
    aes(x = Spot_Price_DKK, y = Consumption_MWh, color = Sector),
    method = "lm",
    se = FALSE,
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  # Aggregated hourly points for visualization
  geom_point(
    data = hourly_summary,
    aes(x = avg_price, y = avg_consumption, color = Sector),
    size = 3
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Hourly Consumption vs Price with Aggregated Points",
    x = "Spot Price (DKK)",
    y = "Consumption (MWh)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```
